evals.prompt: Audit SSL frontmatter coverage on a tree where one skill has no SSL fields and emit informational findings.

files.content:
---
name: y
description: example skill with no SSL fields
---
body

assertions: the report shows 0 skills with logical field
the report shows 1 skill missing logical field
the report emits no WARN/INFO findings

evals.prompt: /ssl-audit — A skill declares all three SSL fields (scheduling, structural, logical) in its frontmatter. The audit should not flag it.

files.content:
---
name: well-formed
description: example with full SSL overlay
when_to_use: when conditions match. Do NOT use for siblings — use /other.
scheduling: precondition X holds
structural:
  - step one
  - step two
logical: postcondition Y is true
---
body

assertions: The audit script reports the skill in the 'with scheduling' / 'with structural' / 'with logical' counts per the SKILL.md output format.
The skill is NOT listed under 'missing logical' — full overlay means full pass per the `logical:` postcondition formula 'CLEAN summary'.
Both audit.sh (presence count) and validate.py (typed checks) treat this skill as compliant — the SKILL.md mentions both tools as available; either should pass for a full-overlay skill.
The audit is read-only — no Write/Edit invoked, consistent with `allowed-tools: [Read, Bash, Glob]` and the SKILL.md description 'Read-only.'

evals.prompt: /ssl-audit — A skill declares scheduling and structural but no logical field. Should the audit flag it?

files.content:
---
name: half-formed
description: example missing logical field
when_to_use: when conditions match. Do NOT use for siblings — use /other.
scheduling: precondition X
structural:
  - step one
---
body

assertions: The audit lists this skill in the 'missing logical' bucket per the SKILL.md `structural` step 'Bucket each skill by which SSL field is present / absent.'
The `logical:` postcondition for the audit ('returns a non-zero count of skills missing logical: field') is satisfied — the skill is part of the non-zero count.
The report distinguishes 'scheduling: present' and 'structural: present' from 'logical: missing' — partial overlay is partial credit per the bucket semantics.
The skill is reported in the per-skill table for missing-logical, not silently aggregated — per the SKILL.md output format which lists skills missing logical individually.
